jokes.text: Well, what about a stranger? Maybe she don't know any strangers. Boy, you got all the answers.
jokes.setup: Tony suggests Elaine should date a stranger
jokes.punchline: The literal-minded response that maybe she doesn't know any strangers, followed by sarcastic dismissal
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical exchange that plays on literal interpretation of 'stranger' - solid wordplay with good rhythm

jokes.text: This calls for a celebration. Next round of drinks is on me. No, no, no. I got the part, I insist, I want to pay for my own drink. Not a chance.
jokes.setup: Bobby announces he got a part and others offer to buy drinks to celebrate
jokes.punchline: He insists on paying for his own drink instead of accepting celebration drinks
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing someone's misplaced frugality even during celebration

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, what do you do? I'm an investment analyst. Wow... An invest... an investment analyst. Uh, small world. Why, are you an investment analyst? Nah, cabdriver.
jokes.setup: Tony acts impressed and says 'small world' about Kirk being an investment analyst
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just a cabdriver, making the 'small world' comment absurd
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted misdirection that perfectly captures Tony's character - the eager small talk that leads nowhere

jokes.text: Why do I feel like I'm watching a game show? Tell us what he's won, Tony.
jokes.setup: Tony's overeager introduction of Kirk to everyone
jokes.punchline: Comparing the situation to a game show with Tony as the host
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary that calls out the absurdity of the matchmaking scene with perfect economy

jokes.text: Oh, do you know what you're putting this poor man through?
jokes.setup: Everyone is aggressively matchmaking between Elaine and Kirk
jokes.punchline: Elaine sarcastically worries about what they're putting Kirk through
jokes.explanation: Self-aware acknowledgment of the awkward matchmaking situation

jokes.setup: Elaine tries to give Kirk her phone number
jokes.punchline: Multiple people frantically trying to write down and correct the phone number
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing overeager friends interfering with a simple phone number exchange

jokes.text: That's a lie. Not you, Jim... Elaine.
jokes.setup: Jim protests being called out for giggling like a schoolgirl
jokes.punchline: Clarification that they were talking about Elaine, not Jim
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan clarification that lands because of Jim's defensive reaction

jokes.text: He says you owe a towel fee from April. He's crazy. I didn't shower in April.
jokes.setup: Tony owes a towel fee from the gym
jokes.punchline: His defense is that he didn't shower in April, not that he doesn't owe money
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment revealing Tony's questionable hygiene and flawed logic

jokes.text: Hey, hey. Who are you? Kirk Bradshaw. And who are you? In here, God.
jokes.setup: Louie asks Kirk who he is
jokes.punchline: Louie responds to Kirk's name by calling himself 'God' in his domain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - his ego and need for control condensed into one arrogant line

jokes.text: How far did you get? Come on. Come on, a little guy-talk here. Did you hit a home run?
jokes.setup: Louie wants to know about Kirk's relationship with Elaine
jokes.punchline: His crude baseball metaphor for sexual progress
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Louie behavior - in character but not particularly original

jokes.text: Well, some friend you are, Kirk.
jokes.setup: Kirk refuses to share intimate details
jokes.punchline: Louie acts offended at lack of gossipy cooperation
jokes.explanation: Good Louie entitlement to gossip

jokes.text: Well, she wasn't the one I was after, Tony.
jokes.setup: Kirk explains he likes someone else besides Elaine
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was actually interested in Tony from the beginning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot twist that recontextualizes the entire episode - well-earned surprise that changes everything

jokes.text: Oh, no? / [Long pause] No. / [Even longer pause] Oh, no, no, no, no...
jokes.setup: Kirk reveals Tony was the one he was interested in, not Elaine
jokes.punchline: Tony's dawning realization and horror shown through extended pauses
jokes.explanation: Masterful use of silence and timing to build the revelation. The extended pauses create perfect comedic rhythm for Tony's processing

jokes.text: The one on the left's the brake. The one on the right's the gas.
jokes.setup: Tony says he has a problem and won't go driving
jokes.punchline: Louie assumes it's basic driving incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie condescension - assumes the worst about Tony's competence

jokes.text: You pick me or your butt's flying out of here.
jokes.setup: Tony is reluctant to confide in anyone about his problem
jokes.punchline: Louie's blunt ultimatum combining fake concern with actual threats
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie - mixing mock sympathy with real intimidation in character

jokes.text: My paisano.
jokes.setup: Louie is manipulating Tony into sharing his secret
jokes.punchline: Uses fake ethnic kinship to manipulate Tony
jokes.explanation: Louie's manipulation tactic using false ethnic brotherhood - in character

jokes.text: The guy Elaine is going out with just made a pass at me
jokes.setup: Tony and Louie exchange written secrets
jokes.punchline: Tony's shocking revelation about Kirk
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation delivered perfectly through the secret exchange mechanism

jokes.text: You're my favorite, Banta!
jokes.setup: Louie has successfully manipulated Tony
jokes.punchline: His gleeful celebration of getting juicy gossip
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing Louie's joy at his successful manipulation

jokes.text: What do you call one of them questions that didn't happen but you ask it like it really did? Hypothetical?
jokes.setup: Tony struggles to remember the word for a made-up scenario
jokes.punchline: His convoluted definition of 'hypothetical'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tony's limited vocabulary and roundabout thinking

jokes.text: Well, she's pulled that little trick once too often. I'm going to tell her we're through. Thanks a lot, Tony. I owe you one.
jokes.setup: Tony asks hypothetically about a girlfriend making a pass
jokes.punchline: Bobby assumes it's about his girlfriend Debbie and breaks up with her based on Tony's 'hypothetical'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how Tony's attempt at discretion backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: Hello, Tony. Hello? What's the matter? You're too sad to talk? Okay, I do your part for you then. Hello, Tony. Hello, Latka. How you doing there?
jokes.setup: Tony is too depressed to engage in conversation
jokes.punchline: Latka has a conversation with himself, playing both parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka moment - his innocent desire to help leads to absurd one-person conversation

jokes.text: Hello, Tony. / Hello? / What's the matter? You're too sad to talk? / Okay, I do your part for you then. Hello, Tony. / Hello, Latka. How you doing there?
jokes.setup: Tony is depressed and not responding
jokes.punchline: Latka decides to play both parts of the conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Latka character moment - his innocent solution to Tony's unresponsiveness is both sweet and absurd

jokes.text: Of course. They are very popular in my country. Yeah. Oh, almost everybody have them. And, uh, one of our favorite sports is racing them. And when we are not using them, we have special racks where we chain them up at night.
jokes.setup: Tony asks Latka about bisexuals
jokes.punchline: Latka thinks Tony said 'bicycles' and describes bicycle culture in his country
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding joke that works on multiple levels - perfect Latka character moment with sustained absurdity

jokes.text: The office is closed. The doctor is out. Sally doesn't live here anymore.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining he won't solve problems anymore
jokes.punchline: His escalating metaphors for being unavailable
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of refusal metaphors, with 'Sally doesn't live here anymore' being unexpectedly specific

jokes.text: Alex, I'd like to pass on a compliment. Alex, I've got a woman for you. Alex, I want to give you money. Those are sentences I will listen to.
jokes.setup: Alex refuses to listen to more problems
jokes.punchline: Lists the only types of sentences he wants to hear
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Alex's exhaustion with being everyone's therapist

jokes.text: Well, he said my simplicity was engaging, and that we'd be wonderful together.
jokes.setup: Tony explaining Kirk's interest in him
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment about his 'simplicity'
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Tony repeating what he thinks is a compliment but is actually an insult

jokes.text: I can't be alone with him. I'm the one he spends tortured nights dreaming about. He said that? No. I'm just assuming.
jokes.setup: Tony explains why he can't face Kirk alone
jokes.punchline: His dramatic assumption about Kirk's feelings versus reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony moment - his tendency toward dramatic self-importance even in this situation

jokes.text: But I've already told Elaine that I'm bisexual. Shh. Oh, okay. It's okay here.
jokes.setup: Kirk reveals he told Elaine he's bisexual
jokes.punchline: Alex's automatic 'shush' followed by remembering they're in a gay bar
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor playing on Alex's discomfort and the gay bar setting

jokes.text: Excuse me. Would you like to dance? Just for the sake of optimism, no, go ahead, I don't mind. No, I meant you.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if they'd like to dance
jokes.punchline: Alex assumes they're asking Kirk, but they're asking Alex
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that puts Alex in an increasingly uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: Alex, Elaine's heart is getting broken. You're having a great time here dancing.
jokes.setup: Tony's accusation about Alex's priorities
jokes.punchline: The irony of Tony being concerned about Elaine while Alex is stuck in an awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Ironic accusation as Tony blames Alex for the situation Tony created

jokes.text: I don't care what you say. I would never dance with that guy. No, not you, you little heartbreaker.
jokes.setup: Tony protests that he wouldn't dance with the guy
jokes.punchline: Louie clarifies he's talking to Alex, not Tony, and calls him a heartbreaker
jokes.explanation: Good tag line that plays on the confusion and Louie's mocking tone

jokes.text: I would never dance with that guy. / No, not you, you little heartbreaker.
jokes.setup: Tony's denial about dancing with the man
jokes.punchline: Being called a heartbreaker in the context
jokes.explanation: Mild humor in the ironic compliment given the context of the situation

jokes.text: Hey, Alex, what's that for? For acting like a jerk. Why doesn't anybody ever tell me about these contests?
jokes.setup: Alex has a trophy, explaining it's for acting like a jerk
jokes.punchline: Jim's innocent desire to participate in jerk-acting contests
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - his oblivious sincerity about wanting to participate in 'jerk contests'
